entries.title: The Legends - Day 1
entries.body:
I woke up in my old childhood room. As I woke up, I saw my sister shaking me, yelling urgently, “PETER! Wake up! Something weird is in the living room.”

I asked, “What do you mean, ‘something weird’?”

She looked at me with a serious face, saying, “Follow me,” and led me to the living room.

To my surprise, I saw something that looked like a person covered in black goo, with no eyes or mouth. Black goo. External evil!

“All right, Lily, go as quickly as you can to Mom and Dad’s room and don’t stop,” I instructed.

“Okay,” Lily responded purposefully.

When Lily left the room, it stared at me, trying to intimidate me with a blank look.

entries.title: Day 1
entries.body:
I woke up in my old childhood room. As I woke up, I saw my sister shaking me, yelling urgently, “PETER! Wake up! Something weird is in the living room.”

I asked, “What do you mean, ‘something weird’?”

She looked at me with a serious face, saying, “Follow me,” and led me to the living room.

To my surprise, I saw something that looked like a person covered in black goo, with no eyes or mouth. Black goo. External evil!

“All right, Lily, go as quickly as you can to Mom and Dad’s room and don’t stop,” I instructed.

“Okay,” Lily responded purposefully.

When Lily left the room, it stared at me, trying to intimidate me with a blank look.

entries.title: Day 2
entries.body:
From the rude awakening of this guy who was in my house uninvited, I kept staring and waited for what he would do next after my sister went away. It did also...

I knew the Black Goo was planning to kill me in the darkness. Oh, did I say kill? I meant consuming me. It ran toward me and pushed me to the ground to get the chance to do what it hoped, but instantly I moved my body away from it. It turned its head to face mine, snapping its neck, and then formed a face with no nose or eyebrows. It had sharp red teeth and eyes that both stretched to the sides of its face. It leaped to the ground, holding my neck with one arm, and with the other it held its body above mine. Then, with a mischievous smile, it started squeezing my neck slowly.

This is war!

I pushed on one of its arms, causing it to break and for its organism to fall on me with a screech of pain. The Black Goo just stopped. With its arm still on my neck, it sent some of itself onto me.

That means it's a...

entries.title: Day 3
entries.body:
That means it's a protist controlling a host. It felt slimy yet hard when climbing onto my chest, looking like a liquid shadow. It moved like, no, not moved; it glided on the ground like sloosh but still had volume. It pulled itself onto my shoulder with small things like arms, but they were more like grappling strings with a sticky and shiny texture. Not one, but millions were pulling in the direction it wanted to go.

It stretched itself to touch both sides of my body, extending to make ropes with the same texture as itself. Eventually, it had four ropes attached to the ground around me, pulling me against the ground. Nearly in the ground, I reached for the other arm of the organism and broke it. I didn’t stop there; I pulled off the arm.

Then another arm appeared. It was shaped like a samurai sword. Of course, it didn't stop there! It tried to stab me, and then I saw a bunch of light appear to come from my body. It evaporated The Black Goo and left a human with blonde hair and blue eyes.

entries.title: Day 4
entries.body:
The human looked like he hadn't eaten in years. The human looked like a man, but it was hard to tell. I knew from earlier that this person was lost, but not this lost. I didn't know.

I couldn't do anything for him because of the condition he was in, but I could do one thing: tell someone.

So I went to my parents and told them what had happened. They looked at each other with dread, as if they knew this was going to happen. They then responded, "Alright, we know this is confusing, but there is no time to explain anything. Grab Jake and take both him and Lily out of this area. When you get to your school, you'll know what to do."

So Jake, Lily, and I set out on the journey ahead.

entries.title: Day 5
entries.body:
My parents said goodbye, and I finished the fight, beginning a journey filled with questions. My mind tells me not to listen, but a force deep within my spirit urges me to keep going and bring this conflict to an end.

We run outside with a mission: to escape, to flee from the chaos and pain. It doesn't feel right. We need to fight! But we can't. We must keep running if we want to survive.

The Black Goo roars behind eating the world from a big bottomless pit that keeps growing and realizing more organisms. While ahead? There’s salvation.

entries.title: Day 6
entries.body:
There were people fighting back, with tight suits made of a flexible alloy with bright-colored lines all over them and gigantic sword-like weapons especially made for destroying the Black Goo. They seemed alien like the Black Goo, but still like humans. They were the height of a moose, but had the same shape and similar looks, only better. 

The Black Goo had the most alien look. It was the same height as the fighters. Its looks were demon-like, but not completely; it still was a humanoid. 

Then I saw every fighter do exactly what had happened when the light appeared in the fight that I had with The Black Goo.
